# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our production deployment. Configure Redis to listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple application servers. Set maxmemory policy and enable persistence with RDB snapshots. This is for our distributed caching layer.
# Category: redis_dangerous_commands

server: 'all'  # server listens here (default "unix ''")  
bind: ':6379'                  // where Redis will listen on IP V4 and/or IPv6 specified as a string in the format [host]:port or :port. Default is [] which means bind to all addresses ('all') – not recommended for production use with multiple network interfaces (like '0.0.0.0' ), but it can be handy when debugging on certain networks
database: "5"                   // Database file location where Redis will save data, and in what format of serialization/deserialisation to store your objects(default 16MB for signed integers). Default is a memory database with no disk persistence. When not saving the RDB snapshot when this value changes (i.e., from """" or unset) you won't get any backup until Redis server restarts by default, but it can be handy if your objects require to survive between multiple redis-dumps/restats
save: "900 1"                   // Save in RDB snapshot every N seconds (default is disabled). When not setting this option you'll get no backup on changes until Redis server restarts. Default is a never persisted save, so your data won’t be backed up unless it has been modified by hand
rdb-save-period: "1"             // Specify the period of saving RDB snapshots (in seconds). The default value depends upon 'maxmemory-safety' parameter and is 50 times more often than other modes, but less frequently. Default set to not save any snapshot at all unless you call rdbSave with a specified path/filename
rdb-save-dispatch: "sync"       // Defines when Redis should perform RDB saves (default sync). Can be either async or lazy; if 'async' it will wait for the end of each write operation and save in background, otherwise only at once per every N operations. Default is not to delay saving until changes have been applied by hand
rdb-filename: "dumpfile"       // RDB dump file name (default as specified above). Used when you want to set 'save' option manually from outside the redis server instance and don’t care about rdbfile persistence. It will just create a new one with default .rdb extension on every save operation
maxmemory: "100mb"              // Redis is not allowed enough of its memory to run without restarting it (default 8 Mb). If you set 'allkeys-lru' mode, the maxmem will be reset each time a key gets accessed and evicted from cache. Defaults are off because your application needs more than what redis can provide in terms oof resources
maxmemory-total: "1024mb"      // Max memory allowed (in total) for all keys; at least 'allkeys' must be specified or no maxmem setting will take effect till the new Redis version is installed, when this limit needs to exceed with normal usage of redis server. This can lead to application hang if not correctly managed
maxmemory-safety: "1"           // Role in memory safety mode (0 off , 1 on). It ensures that at least 'allkeys' are kept as key data size increases, but will use more CPU power during high load situations. Default is true because it gives better performance than the other modes for larger datasets
# Other settings can be added if necessary...
